title: Diagram Viewer v1 Rebuild
content:
Rebuild the v0 D2 Diagram Viewer as `<diagram-viewer>` — a multi-file Web Component, bundled with `go tool esbuild`, served by Caddy, persisted in localStorage with JSON import/export, and verified by a full Playwright-go end-to-end suite — mirroring the story-mapper sibling project structure.

## Decomposition

Parent custom element `<diagram-viewer>` plus three sub-components:

- `<diagram-canvas>` — iframe rendering, SVG/PNG dimension detection, PNG-with-SVG-overlay handling, zoom (keys + mouse wheel), iframe link interception → `slide-navigate` event
- `<diagram-nav-tree>` — sidebar header, recursive nav tree of `layers/children/steps`, step bullets, active highlighting, click → `slide-select` event, sidebar collapse + resize
- `<diagram-help-modal>` — native `<dialog>` with keyboard shortcut reference

## Manifest format

Preserved verbatim from v0: `{ name?, title?, version?, generated?, description?, layers: [{ id, title, path, type, children?, steps?, overlay? }] }`. `loadData()` accepts this shape only; the README `{ images: [...] }` wrapper is out of scope for v1.

## localStorage persistence

Single versioned snapshot under storage key `diagramViewer.v1`:

- `version: 1`
- `manifest` (full v0-shape object or null when loaded by URL only)
- `basePath`
- `ui: { currentSlideId, zoomPercent, sidebarOpen, sidebarWidthPx }`

Stores both UI state and the full manifest so users can paste a manifest into the JSON dialog without serving any files, and a reload restores the exact view including the manifest.

## Public API

- `loadData(data)` — accept v0 shape, reject otherwise; preserves UI state when called over an existing snapshot
- `reset()` — clear localStorage and re-apply original source

## JSON import/export dialog

Native `<dialog>` with `<textarea>` + `Copy` / `Apply` / `Close` + inline error region. Apply does full-replace (not merge); rejects parse errors and `version !== 1`.

## Build & serve

- `go.mod` Go 1.26+ with `tool` directives for esbuild + caddy (mirrors story-mapper)
- `Taskfile.yaml`: `build`, `dev`, `test`, `serve`, `clean`
- `Caddyfile` on `:8080` with permissive CORS
- esbuild bundles `src/diagram-viewer.js` → `dist/diagram-viewer.js` and `src/styles.css` → `dist/diagram-viewer.css`
- `index.html` at root with `<diagram-viewer>` + inline module script that fetches `examples/kubernetes/manifest.json` and calls `loadData(data)`

## No-regression requirement

All v0 behaviour must survive the decomposition: keyboard nav (Up/Down/Left/Right/Space/Home/End/=/-/0/f/?/Esc), URL-hash sync, attribute-driven initial state (`manifest`, `base-path`, `sidebar`, `zoom`, `start-at`), sidebar resize handle, fullscreen toggle, error states for missing/invalid manifest, iframe link interception, SVG-overlay-on-PNG rendering.

## Tests

One Playwright-go test file per component, all sharing a single `TestMain` and helper set in `tests/main_test.go`:

- `tests/diagram_viewer_test.go` — parent: loadData accept/reject, JSON dialog export/import/error, Reset, persistence-across-reload
- `tests/diagram_canvas_test.go` — canvas: zoom keys, mouse-wheel zoom, SVG dimensions, PNG-with-overlay, iframe link interception, ArrowLeft history-back
- `tests/diagram_nav_tree_test.go` — sidebar: nav click, expand/collapse, sidebar collapse/toggle, resize-and-persist, keyboard nav
- `tests/diagram_help_modal_test.go` — help: ? open, Esc close, close button, full shortcut list rendered

Each test file targets the user-visible surface of its component, exercised through the real DOM.

## References

- v0: `/Users/metafeather/Developer/src/metafeather.net/codelabs/d2/viewer/`
- Sibling project mirrored: `/Users/metafeather/Developer/src/metafeather.net/metafeather-org/story-mapper/`
- Plan: `TODO.md` at project root

title: Diagram Viewer v1 — post-rebuild bug fixes
content: Independent workflow for the post-rebuild bug-fix iteration of <diagram-viewer>. Sibling to s-9gls (the original v1 rebuild). Six user-reported bugs after the v1 rebuild landed: nested-heading highlight bleeds to the parent in the sidebar, sticky :focus blue outline after click + keyboard nav, PNG/SVG-overlay misalignment on cloud-controller-manager, JSON dialog centred to viewport instead of host, JSON button in wrong toolbar (top instead of sidebar footer), and Reset button having no visible effect. Plus: move both JSON and Reset to the sidebar footer and remove the top toolbar entirely. See TODO.md at project root for the task breakdown. Implementation tracked under a parent epic with one child issue per bug, plus a leaf tests issue providing summary feedback on close.

title: Diagram Viewer v1 — bug-fix regressions (Bug 2 & 3 rework)
content: Post-implementation regression workflow for two bugs whose original fixes (under spec s-448f) did not fully resolve the user-visible issue. Bug 2 (sticky :focus blue outline): the original fix added :focus-visible CSS but never blurred the clicked link, so any subsequent arrow-key press promotes the still-focused link to :focus-visible and the blue outline returns on the previously-clicked heading. Bug 3 (PNG/SVG-overlay misalignment on cloud-controller-manager): the original fix injected zero-margin styles via iframeDoc.head — but Chrome does NOT wrap a directly-loaded SVG in HTML, so iframeDoc.head is null, the property access throws, the outer try/catch in #handleIframeLoad falls through to setDefaultDimensions(), and the iframe is sized to 100% × 100% of the canvas, leaving the SVG content rendered tiny at top-left while the PNG contain-fits to the much-bigger iframe. See TODO.md (Discovered Tasks section) for the three-task breakdown. Sibling to s-448f and s-9gls.

title: Diagram Viewer multi-instance encapsulation
content:
Allow more than one <diagram-viewer> on the same page, each loadable, navigable, zoomable, and resettable independently — with no shared localStorage state, no URL-hash collisions, no document-level keyboard cross-talk, and no leaked styles or globals.

## Failing → Passing criterion

A new examples/multi.html page with two viewers and a new Playwright suite that exercises independence. Pre-fix the suite must FAIL on shared localStorage, hash collisions, keyboard cross-talk, and double-define safety. Post-fix every test passes.

## Scope

1. Per-instance identity (#instanceId): id attr → FNV-1a hash of (manifest, base-path) → crypto.randomUUID().
2. Namespaced localStorage (diagramViewer.v1:${instanceId}); reset() only removes own key; one-time legacy migration when single-instance.
3. Opt-in URL hash via 'bookmarkable' attribute; 'primary' breaks ties when multiple bookmarkable viewers exist.
4. Scope keyboard to :focus-within (replace mouseenter/mouseleave + document keydown).
5. Eliminate residual global escapes — globalThis.focus(), persistent document mousemove/mouseup, and unguarded customElements.define calls.
6. Multi-instance demo: examples/multi.html.
7. Multi-instance Playwright tests proving independence (red→green criterion).

## Workflow

Implementation is tracked by epic i-9tby (which 'implements' this spec). The 7 tasks form a linear blocks chain so 'sudocode ready' surfaces exactly one task at a time:

  i-20ih → i-139y → i-5zjh → i-6r8b → i-7is1 → i-6xqh → i-2ess → i-9tby

At any moment exactly one issue should be ready; closing it makes the next ready, until the leaf (i-2ess) closes.

## Closure protocol

1. Tasks 1–6 (i-20ih … i-6xqh) close in chain order as their work completes.
2. When the leaf i-2ess (Playwright tests) closes:
   - the feedback anchor at line 3 ('Failing → Passing criterion') fires summary feedback onto this spec;
   - i-9tby (epic) becomes ready (no remaining blockers).
3. Close i-9tby once the feedback on this spec is reviewed and the failing→passing criterion is met.
4. Close this spec (s-7ubt) once i-9tby is closed and the feedback has been resolved or dismissed (sudocode feedback dismiss).

## Notes

- bookmarkable is OFF by default — documented breaking change for v1 single-instance users.
- Two viewers with identical (manifest, base-path) and no id share storage by design; users wanting independence give each an id.
- customElements.define is guarded across all four component files: re-loading the bundle is a no-op; conflicting constructors log a single console.warn rather than throwing.
- Shadow DOM + adoptedStyleSheets already isolate CSS from the page; the CSS-leak test is a regression guard, not a fix.
